definition: Computer Aided Dispatch. Systems used by dispatchers to track status and location of resources.
Full-Time Equivalent. A unit that indicates the workload of an employed person.
Next Generation 9-1-1. An initiative to update the 9-1-1 service infrastructure to improve public emergency communications services in a wireless mobile society.
Public Safety Answering Point. A facility equipped and staffed to receive 9-1-1 calls.
Service Level Agreement. A commitment between a service provider and a client.
Emergency calls received on a standard 10-digit phone line rather than the 9-1-1 network.
Teletypewriter / Telecommunications Device for the Deaf.
